risks.description: Stale-claim regression during rewrite: rewriting one doc accidentally introduces a new stale claim about another phase's behavior (e.g., rewriting SCHEMA.md against current ElementData but referencing old DependencyGraph terminology in a graph-schema cross-reference).
risks.mitigation: Each doc edit cites its source file in the diff message. Reviewer checks every claim against the cited source. AC-09 stale_claim_inventory close-out forces explicit status per item — no item left unaddressed.

risks.description: Doc-vs-code drift discovery mid-implementation: while rewriting a doc to match shipped behavior, the agent finds the code doesn't actually match its own architecture (e.g., a documented behavior is wrong, but reality is also wrong in a different way). Phase 8 has zero code-fix budget.
risks.mitigation: Hard rule: any code-vs-code conflict files a stub through the normal flow, does NOT inline-fix. Plan task lists 'discover-stub-and-continue' explicitly as a halt-and-report point.

risks.description: BNF mirror drift: docs/HEADER-GRAMMAR.md (CORE) and SKILLS/ANALYSIS/analyze-coderef-semantics/SKILL.md (ASSISTANT) diverge over time because the mirror is manual. Future header grammar changes touch ASSISTANT but forget CORE.
risks.mitigation: Mirror doc states the canonical source explicitly with file path + commit-pin. Future header-grammar WOs add 'update CORE mirror' as a closeout task. Risk is post-Phase-8 maintenance, not Phase 8 itself — file follow-up stub if drift detection automation is wanted.

risks.description: MIGRATION.md backward-compatibility claims that no longer hold: pipeline rebuild changed scanner identity, relationship extraction, graph schema. MIGRATION.md may promise migration paths from versions that pre-date these changes and now point to dead code.
risks.mitigation: Survey MIGRATION.md early in prep. If full rewrite needed, AC-09 covers it via stale_claim_inventory. If migration paths are now obsolete (no users on those paths), candidate for archival to docs/archive/MIGRATION-pre-rebuild.md with banner.

risks.description: Audit-style root markdown overcommitment: EXECUTIVE-SUMMARY.md, AUDIT-REPORT.md, CODEREF-ANALYSIS-REPORT.md are point-in-time snapshots. Treating each as a doc to keep current would balloon Phase 8 scope and produce permanently-stale-tomorrow outputs.
risks.mitigation: DR-PHASE-8-A locks disposition: archive vs delete vs banner. Phase 8 does NOT promise to keep audit-style snapshots current — they are dated artifacts of the rebuild itself.

risks.description: README.md cross-link rot: README.md often acts as a table-of-contents pointing into docs/. After Phase 8 rewrites/renames/archives several docs, README's links break.
risks.mitigation: Plan task explicitly: 'README.md cross-link audit' as one of the last Phase 8 tasks (after all docs/ rewrites land). Use a markdown link-check tool (or grep) to find broken links.

risks.description: Boundary enforcer false-positive risk if a doc-content example accidentally matches the regex. The enforcer test scans TypeScript source files for the 8 forbidden field names with regex `\b{name}\s*[:?=]`. Markdown isn't scanned, so the risk is low — but if a Phase 8 task eventually proposes a TypeScript snippet INSIDE a doc that triggers the regex by some toolchain quirk, the enforcer could fire.
risks.mitigation: Risk is theoretical (the enforcer scans .ts files, not .md). Document in plan: if ever the enforcer fires on Phase 8 work, halt-and-report — do NOT relax the regex.

decision_records.question: What disposition for the audit-style root markdown files (EXECUTIVE-SUMMARY.md, AUDIT-REPORT.md, CODEREF-ANALYSIS-REPORT.md, DUPLICATE-FILES-AUDIT.md, GENERATE_FOUNDATION_DOCS_ALIGNMENT_PLAN.md)?
decision_records.decision: Archive to docs/archive/<filename>-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md with a one-line dated banner explaining the snapshot is preserved for historical reference. Do NOT keep them in root, do NOT delete them. Banner text: 'This document captures the project state at <date>; for current state see <pointer>.'
decision_records.rationale: These are valuable historical artifacts of the rebuild process — deletion loses institutional memory. Keeping them in root creates the appearance they're current truth. Archival with explicit dated banners preserves history without misleading readers.
alternatives_considered: Leave in root with banner — clutters root and risks reader confusion; rejected.
Move to docs/archive/ without banner — confusing without context; rejected.

decision_records.question: One-source-per-topic vs distributed-with-cross-links for the schema/contract docs (scanner schema, relationship schema, graph schema, resolution statuses)?
decision_records.decision: One-source-per-topic. Each schema gets ONE canonical doc file. Other docs cross-link rather than duplicate. SCHEMA.md becomes the canonical location for scanner-schema and relationship-schema (sibling sections). Graph schema gets its own GRAPH-SCHEMA.md (or graph-schema section in SCHEMA.md — TBD by prep). API.md cross-links into SCHEMA.md rather than restating types.
decision_records.rationale: Distributed duplication is the primary source of drift. A single canonical location makes the truth source obvious and makes future maintenance one-place-to-update. Cross-linking is cheap; copy-paste is expensive over time.
alternatives_considered: Distributed with cross-links — what we have today, drift-prone; rejected.
Massive single SCHEMA.md covering everything — too long to navigate; rejected.
One file per type (per Phase, per layer) — fragments truth; rejected.

decision_records.question: How does the CORE-side header grammar doc relate to the canonical BNF in ASSISTANT/SKILLS/ANALYSIS/analyze-coderef-semantics/SKILL.md?
decision_records.decision: CORE-side doc is a MIRROR with explicit citation. Top of file declares: 'Mirror of canonical BNF at <full path to ASSISTANT SKILL.md>. Authoritative source is the ASSISTANT-side document; this CORE-side mirror exists for self-contained CORE doc builds. Sync touchpoint: any change to the ASSISTANT BNF must update this mirror in the same WO closeout.'
decision_records.rationale: CORE consumers shouldn't have to traverse to ASSISTANT to read the grammar that CORE's parser implements, but ASSISTANT remains the canonical authority. Mirror-with-citation gives both: convenience and clear authority. Sync-touchpoint convention forces future grammar changes to keep the mirror current.
alternatives_considered: Live-include via tooling — overkill for a doc; rejected.
No CORE-side doc, link only — bad UX for CORE readers; rejected.
CORE becomes the authority and ASSISTANT mirrors — wrong direction (BNF originated in ASSISTANT skill); rejected.

decision_records.question: How is the agent usage contract structured (separate AGENTS.md vs section in API.md vs new docs/AGENT-USAGE.md)?
decision_records.decision: Use existing AGENTS.md at repo root as the canonical agent usage contract. Rewrite to cover: (a) what to read (validation-report.json, IndexingResult.status, exit codes), (b) what is internal pipeline state not for agent consumption, (c) the gate contract (validation-report.ok=false → indexing refuses), (d) IndexingResult.status semantics, (e) version compatibility commitments. AGENTS.md is well-named for the audience and already exists.
decision_records.rationale: Repurposing existing well-named file beats creating a new one. AGENTS.md is the obvious place an agent or agent-author looks. CLAUDE.md and GEMINI.md (also at root) become Claude/Gemini-specific addenda that link to AGENTS.md as the base contract.
alternatives_considered: New docs/AGENT-USAGE.md — duplicates AGENTS.md role; rejected.
Section in API.md — buries the contract; rejected.
Triple split AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/GEMINI.md as siblings — loses the canonical-base-contract pattern; rejected.

decision_records.question: CHANGELOG.md format — keep current format or migrate to Keep-a-Changelog?
decision_records.decision: Keep current format. Phase 8 adds a single new entry summarizing the doc rebuild and a 'Pipeline Rebuild Complete (Phase 0..7)' line citing archive paths. Format migration is out-of-scope; format consistency with prior entries wins over format upgrade.
decision_records.rationale: Format migration is its own concern, decoupled from Phase 8's doc-truth-up mission. Mixing them would expand scope and risk. If Keep-a-Changelog migration is wanted later, it gets its own WO.
alternatives_considered: Migrate to Keep-a-Changelog now — scope creep; rejected.
Skip CHANGELOG entirely — rebuild milestone deserves a changelog entry; rejected.

phase_8_completion_criteria: All 7 required docs (roadmap line 455) exist at their canonical locations and reflect current code behavior

no source code changes outside docs/, root *.md, and CHANGELOG.md (verify via git diff at close)

phase_8_boundary._purpose: Explicit list of fields, files, and behaviors that belong to Phase 9+ (which does NOT exist) or to ongoing maintenance. Phase 8 must NOT touch these.
phase_8_boundary._note: There is no Phase 9. After Phase 8, the rebuild is complete. The items below are explicitly out-of-scope for Phase 8 even if the urge arises during implementation.
no_leak_items: Source code behavior changes (zero tolerance — file stub, do not fix)
New test files beyond doc-presence checks (the boundary enforcer is sufficient; do not add more)
New CLI flags (Phase 7 closed the CLI surface for the rebuild)
New schema fields (none — Phase 5 + Phase 7 closed the type surfaces)
ASSISTANT-side skill changes (ASSISTANT skills can read new docs, but doc-update WO does not modify skills)
Test infrastructure changes (no-phase-8-docs-leak.test.ts is the only doc-related test, and it shipped in Phase 7)

real_world_baseline._phase_8_use: These numbers should appear in updated docs (e.g., README.md, CHANGELOG.md) as illustration of the post-rebuild state. Doc rewrites should NOT invent baseline numbers.
